commands.name: sw_vers
commands.description: Print macOS product name, version, and build (system profiler shortcut)

patterns.text: see which macOS version you are running
patterns.explanation: Shows product name, version, and build number

patterns.text: print only the macOS version number for scripts
patterns.explanation: Outputs a single line like 14.2.1 suitable for automation

patterns.text: get the build number for troubleshooting or compatibility checks
patterns.explanation: Shows the internal build identifier (e.g. 23C71)

commands.name: diskutil
commands.description: Manage local disks, volumes, and APFS containers on macOS

patterns.text: list all disks and partitions with identifiers
patterns.explanation: Shows disk0, synthesized volumes, and APFS structure

patterns.text: see detailed information about one disk or volume
patterns.explanation: Prints filesystem type, size, mount point, and UUID

patterns.text: safely eject an external drive or disk image
patterns.explanation: Unmounts and powers down the device when possible

patterns.text: repair permissions and verify a volume (check first)
patterns.explanation: Runs a read-only check on the boot volume without repairing

patterns.text: erase and reformat a USB stick as ExFAT with a label
patterns.explanation: Destructively repartitions disk4; triple-check the device node first

commands.name: launchctl
commands.description: Interface to launchd for user and system services on macOS

patterns.text: list all loaded user launch agents
patterns.explanation: Shows PID, exit status, and label for GUI session services

patterns.text: unload a user LaunchAgent plist you are debugging
patterns.explanation: Stops the job and removes it from launchd until loaded again

patterns.text: load a LaunchAgent after editing its plist
patterns.explanation: Registers and starts the agent in the current user domain

patterns.text: bootstrap a service in the user domain (modern launchctl on newer macOS)
patterns.explanation: Preferred on recent macOS instead of load for some workflows

patterns.text: print environment variables launchd injects for your session
patterns.explanation: Reads a single variable from the caller's launchd context

commands.name: plutil
commands.description: Convert, lint, and edit Apple property list files

patterns.text: check whether a plist is valid XML or binary
patterns.explanation: Validates syntax and prints OK or an error

patterns.text: convert a binary plist to readable XML
patterns.explanation: Outputs XML to stdout for inspection or diffing

patterns.text: convert XML plist to binary for smaller on-disk size
patterns.explanation: Rewrites the file in binary1 format in place

patterns.text: pretty-print JSON as an XML plist
patterns.explanation: Useful when bridging JSON configs into plist-only tools

commands.name: mdfind
commands.description: Search files using Spotlight metadata indexes

patterns.text: find PDF documents indexed by Spotlight
patterns.explanation: Uses the kind query token to match PDF content type

patterns.text: search only your home folder for a phrase in indexed content
patterns.explanation: Restricts the query to your home directory tree

patterns.text: live search that updates as the index changes
patterns.explanation: Keeps streaming new matches until you interrupt it

commands.name: mdls
commands.description: Display Spotlight metadata attributes for a file

patterns.text: see all metadata Spotlight stores for one file
patterns.explanation: Lists attributes like dates, dimensions, and where from

patterns.text: read a single attribute such as pixel dimensions
patterns.explanation: Filters output to specific keys only

commands.name: ditto
commands.description: Copy directories while preserving resource forks, ACLs, and HFS+ metadata

patterns.text: clone a folder for backup keeping extended attributes
patterns.explanation: Preferred over cp -R for macOS bundles and xattrs

patterns.text: copy an app bundle to another volume safely
patterns.explanation: Preserves code signing extended attributes and bundle structure

patterns.text: show what would be copied without writing files
patterns.explanation: Verbose mode lists each path as it is processed

commands.name: xattr
commands.description: List, read, set, and remove extended attributes on files

patterns.text: list extended attributes on a downloaded file
patterns.explanation: Shows quarantine and other attribute names

patterns.text: remove the quarantine flag so Gatekeeper stops warning
patterns.explanation: Only do this for files you trust; it removes download tagging

patterns.text: clear all extended attributes from a file
patterns.explanation: Strips every xattr; use with care

patterns.text: recursively list attributes in a directory tree
patterns.explanation: Useful when debugging codesign or custom metadata

commands.name: softwareupdate
commands.description: Install macOS system updates from the command line

patterns.text: list available software updates without installing
patterns.explanation: Shows recommended updates and labels

patterns.text: install all recommended updates non-interactively
patterns.explanation: Installs all available items; may require a reboot

patterns.text: download available updates without installing them yet
patterns.explanation: Fetches update packages to disk so you can install later offline or after review

commands.name: osascript
commands.description: Run AppleScript or JavaScript for Automation from the shell

patterns.text: show a notification from a shell script
patterns.explanation: Posts a user notification without opening Script Editor

patterns.text: set the Mac volume to 50 percent
patterns.explanation: Controls system audio via AppleScript

patterns.text: run an AppleScript file
patterns.explanation: Executes a compiled or plain .scpt file

commands.name: networksetup
commands.description: Configure network locations, proxies, and Wi-Fi on macOS

patterns.text: list hardware ports and their device names
patterns.explanation: Maps Ethernet vs Wi-Fi to en0, en1, etc.

patterns.text: turn Wi-Fi power on or off
patterns.explanation: Disables wireless; replace en0 with your Wi-Fi interface

patterns.text: show DNS servers for a service
patterns.explanation: Reads resolver configuration for the named service

commands.name: scutil
commands.description: Query system configuration including DNS and proxy settings

patterns.text: see resolver order and search domains
patterns.explanation: Shows how macOS resolves hostnames including VPN split DNS

patterns.text: inspect active proxy configuration
patterns.explanation: Disables HTTP proxy state useful when debugging connectivity

commands.name: security
commands.description: Manage keychains, certificates, and secure storage from the CLI

patterns.text: list keychains the user search list uses
patterns.explanation: Shows paths to login.keychain-db and others

patterns.text: find a generic password item by account name
patterns.explanation: -w prints only the password to stdout; avoid logging

patterns.text: import a certificate into the login keychain
patterns.explanation: Adds a PEM certificate for apps that use the keychain

commands.name: codesign
commands.description: Sign and verify code signatures on macOS binaries and bundles

patterns.text: verify whether an app is signed and not modified
patterns.explanation: Exits 0 if the bundle signature is intact

patterns.text: display signing identity and entitlements of a binary
patterns.explanation: -d verbose shows Team ID and CDHash; entitlements print to stdout

commands.name: log
commands.description: View and stream unified logging data (substitute for tailing /var/log on modern macOS)

patterns.text: stream live logs filtered by process name
patterns.explanation: Shows unified log messages as they arrive

patterns.text: show logs from the last hour for a subsystem
patterns.explanation: Reads persisted logs with a time window and filter

commands.name: pmset
commands.description: Manage power management settings (sleep, wakeup, assertions)

patterns.text: prevent the mac from sleeping temporarily
patterns.explanation: Creates a power assertion to prevent idle sleep

patterns.text: display current power management settings and battery state
patterns.explanation: Shows the active power management profile and battery percentage

patterns.text: schedule the mac to wake up at a specific time
patterns.explanation: Adds a wake event to the system management controller

commands.name: system_profiler
commands.description: Report system hardware and software configuration

patterns.text: get detailed hardware and software profile of the mac
patterns.explanation: Dumps a comprehensive report of all system components

patterns.text: list all USB devices connected to the mac
patterns.explanation: Shows only the USB device tree and connection details

patterns.text: check battery health and cycle count
patterns.explanation: Filters the power profile to extract the battery cycle count

commands.name: screencapture
commands.description: Take screenshots from the command line

patterns.text: capture the whole screen to a file
patterns.explanation: Saves an image of the entire screen to the specified file

patterns.text: capture a selected portion of the screen interactively
patterns.explanation: Allows the user to click and drag to select an area to capture

patterns.text: copy the screenshot directly to the clipboard
patterns.explanation: Captures the screen to the clipboard instead of a file

commands.name: qlmanage
commands.description: Quick Look Server debug and management tool

patterns.text: preview a file using quick look from the terminal
patterns.explanation: Opens a Quick Look window for the specified file

patterns.text: clear the quick look thumbnail cache
patterns.explanation: Resets the Quick Look memory and disk caches
